summary: City of Madison allows up to 8 chickens with a city chicken license. Roosters and on-site slaughter are prohibited.
prerequisites: Apply within 30 days of getting chickens and renew before January 1 each year.
Complete required property or resident notifications when applicable.
Register with Wisconsin DATCP livestock premises registration before submitting the city license application.
rules: Chickens must have a covered enclosure and stay in the covered or fenced enclosure.
The enclosure must be at least 25 feet from any residential structure on an adjacent lot.
The Zoning Administrator may revoke a license after repeated ordinance violations.

links.detail: Use when notification is required for your property type.

summary: City of Milwaukee allows up to 4 chickens on a residential premise with a permit. Roosters and slaughter are prohibited.
prerequisites: Complete the DNS-362 Chicken Coop Site Plan form.
Submit the site plan and permit fee to the Department of Neighborhood Services.
DNS notifies directly or diagonally abutting property owners before a permit is issued.
rules: Residential premises only; commercial and mixed-use occupancies are not eligible.
Coop and yard together must provide at least 16 square feet per chicken.
Coops cannot be taller than 10 feet or exceed 50 square feet.
Enclosures must be at least 25 feet from any residential structure on an adjacent lot and cannot be in the front yard.
